full_response:
We treat every person as a vertex, and every equation  
p(s₁)/p(s₂)=x as a directed edge whose “length’’ (weight) is x.  
All the information we are ever given is therefore a (changing) graph
whose connected components are exactly the sets of people whose price
ratios are already fixed.  
The classical disjoint–set (union–find) structure maintains
components in α(n) amortised time; all we must do is teach it
multiplicative weights.

Data kept for every vertex v
• parent[v]  the usual union–find parent  
• weight[v]  = p(v)/p(parent[v]) (a positive real); for a root
 weight[root]=1  
• size[v]  size of the tree whose root is v (for union-by-size)

A single extra global field
• compCnt  = current number of connected components

--------------------------------------------------------------------
BASIC ROUTINES
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Find(v)                                        // returns (root,ratio)
    if parent[v]=v return (v,1)                 // v is a root
    (r,ρ) ← Find(parent[v])                     // ρ = p(parent[v])/p(r)
    weight[v] ← weight[v]·ρ                     // p(v)/p(r)
    parent[v] ← r                               // path compression
    return (r,weight[v])

UnionWithRatio(s₁,s₂,x)                        // p(s₁)/p(s₂)=x
    (r₁,w₁) ← Find(s₁)      // w₁ = p(s₁)/p(r₁)
    (r₂,w₂) ← Find(s₂)      // w₂ = p(s₂)/p(r₂)

    if r₁ = r₂                                  // already connected
         return                                 // inputs guaranteed consistent

    // Want new edge r₁ → r₂ with weight  w = p(r₁)/p(r₂)
    // From  (w₁·p(r₁))/(w₂·p(r₂)) = x  ⇒  w = x·w₂/w₁

    if size[r₁] < size[r₂]                      // attach r₁ under r₂
         parent[r₁] ← r₂
         weight[r₁] ← x·w₂/w₁
         size[r₂] += size[r₁]
    else                                         // attach r₂ under r₁
         parent[r₂] ← r₁
         weight[r₂] ← 1/(x·w₂/w₁) = w₁/(x·w₂)
         size[r₁] += size[r₂]

    compCnt ← compCnt − 1

--------------------------------------------------------------------
REQUESTED OPERATIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------
UPDATE(s₁,s₂,x):
        UnionWithRatio(s₁,s₂,x)

GETRATIO(s₁,s₂):
        (r₁,w₁) ← Find(s₁)
        (r₂,w₂) ← Find(s₂)
        if r₁ ≠ r₂   return  “insufficient information”
        else          return  w₁ / w₂            // = p(s₁)/p(s₂)

ENOUGHINFO():
        return (compCnt = 1)

--------------------------------------------------------------------
CORRECTNESS PROOFS
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Lemma 1  After every operation weight[v]=p(v)/p(parent[v]) for
all vertices v.  
Proof by induction on the number of operations.  
• Base: initially parent[v]=v, weight[v]=1, so the lemma holds.  
• Induction step:
  – Find only multiplies weight[v] by the ratio of its old parent
    to the root, hence keeps the invariant.
  – UnionWithRatio attaches one root r under the other root R and
    sets weight[r] exactly to  p(r)/p(R) as derived above; every
    other weight is untouched. ∎

Corollary 1  For every vertex v, after Find(v) the second value
returned is p(v)/p(root(v)).

Lemma 2  GETRATIO returns “insufficient information’’
iff p(s₁)/p(s₂) is not implied by the ratios seen so far; otherwise it
returns the unique correct ratio.  
Proof: If the two people lie in different components no ratio can be
derived, so the answer is “insufficient information’’.  If they lie in
the same component, Lemma 1 lets us express
p(s₁)/p(s₂) = (p(s₁)/p(r))/(p(s₂)/p(r)) = w₁/w₂, which is what the
algorithm returns. ∎

Lemma 3  ENOUGHINFO returns true
iff all ratios are known.  
Proof: All ratios are known ⇔ the graph is connected ⇔
compCnt=1 ⇔ the function returns true. ∎

Theorem  The data structure is correct.  
Proof: Immediate from Lemmas 1–3. ∎

--------------------------------------------------------------------
COMPLEXITY ANALYSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Path compression + union-by-size are untouched, therefore the
standard inverse-Ackermann amortised bounds hold:

• Find               O(α(n)) amortised  
• UPDATE (one union) O(α(n)) amortised  
• GETRATIO           2 Finds ⇒ O(α(n)) amortised  
• ENOUGHINFO         O(1)

Space:  three arrays of length n  ⇒  Θ(n).

--------------------------------------------------------------------
INITIALISATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------
MakeSet():
    for every person v
         parent[v]=v, weight[v]=1, size[v]=1
    compCnt = n

--------------------------------------------------------------------
All three required operations are therefore supported in
amortised O(α(n)) time and Θ(n) space.
